thesis_status_reasoning: All monitored parameters are operating well within safe thresholds with substantial safety margins. Sector 4 pressure at 68.5 PSI is 242% above the critical 20 PSI contamination threshold. The system is handling routine morning demand with only 42% pump load, demonstrating significant reserve capacity. Gravity reserves remain at 98%, cavitation sensors show normal operation, and no protective mechanisms have been triggered.
action_reasoning: System performance is nominal with no indicators approaching concern thresholds. All falsification criteria show comfortable safety margins, and the system is demonstrating the resilient baseline operation the thesis predicts.

key_signals.signal: Sector 4 pressure 68.5 PSI vs 20 PSI critical minimum
Primary pump load at 42% during peak demand subsidence
System flow at 4500 GPM vs 20,000 GPM catastrophic threshold
No cavitation warnings or protective valve activation

biggest_uncertainty: Lack of stress test data showing how the system responds to asymmetric extreme load conditions, which is the core resilience claim of the thesis
